synapseLattice.heading: Compute like biology thinks in silicon

nodes.title: Memristor crossbar arrays
nodes.description: Non-volatile resistive memory cells perform multiply-accumulate in-place — eliminating the von Neumann bottleneck and slashing inference energy by orders of magnitude.
nodes.alt: Microchip wafer with dense transistor grid

nodes.title: Spiking neural processors
nodes.description: Event-driven cores fire only when inputs change — mirroring biological neurons to achieve sub-milliwatt always-on perception for robotics, wearables, and edge sensors.
nodes.alt: Neural network visualization with glowing nodes

nodes.title: In-memory compute engines
nodes.description: Weights live where computation happens — SRAM and ReRAM macros execute matrix ops without shuttling tensors across a memory bus, unlocking 100× bandwidth efficiency.
nodes.alt: Server rack with high-density memory modules

nodes.title: Analog AI accelerators
nodes.description: Continuous-valued MAC arrays run inference in the analog domain — delivering tera-ops per watt for always-listening voice, vision, and sensor fusion workloads.
nodes.alt: Oscilloscope traces showing analog signal processing

nodes.title: On-chip synaptic plasticity
nodes.description: Hardware implements STDP and Hebbian learning rules natively — enabling models that adapt in the field without cloud retraining or weight upload cycles.
nodes.alt: Adaptive learning dashboard with live weight updates

nodes.title: Event-driven neuromorphic bus
nodes.description: Asynchronous address-event representation routes sparse spikes across chiplet meshes — collapsing communication overhead for million-neuron scale fabrics.
nodes.alt: Distributed chiplet architecture diagram on display
